# Agent Assignments: Feature 074 — Baseline-Aware Pipeline

**Generated by**: team-lead
**Date**: 2026-03-31
**Status**: APPROVED WITH CONCERNS
**Source**: `specs/074-baseline-aware-pipeline/tasks.md`

---

## Feasibility Status

| Dimension | Assessment |
|-----------|------------|
| Effort | 36 tasks, 9 waves. Cumulative complexity HIGH, individual task complexity MANAGEABLE. |
| Capacity | 4 agents required. No agent exceeds 40% load. |
| Timeline | Realistic: 8 waves active + 1 rework cycle. MVP: 5 waves. |
| Dependencies | All captured. No external blockers. |

**Verdict**: FEASIBLE

---

### Agent Load Summary

| Agent | Tasks | Load % | Waves Active |
|-------|-------|--------|--------------|
| senior-backend-engineer | 33 | ~92% (spread across 9 waves) | 0-8 |
| tester | 3 | ~8% (Wave 8 only) | 8 |
| code-reviewer | Quality gates | Per-wave checkpoints | 1-8 |
| orchestrator | Wave coordination | Execution management | 0-8 |

**Note on Load Distribution**: This is a knowledge system project where all deliverables are markdown and YAML files authored within agent/skill/schema/template definitions. The senior-backend-engineer handles all implementation tasks because all tasks are structured content authoring within a single domain. The workload is distributed across 9 waves with natural breaks at quality gates. No single wave assigns more than 7 tasks to the senior-backend-engineer, and parallel tasks within a wave target independent files.

## Parallel Execution Waves

### Wave 0: Schema Setup
**Effort**: S (Small) | **Quality Gate**: Schema validation

| Task | Agent | Parallel? | File Target |
|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| T001 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | schemas/coverage-checklists.yaml |
| T002 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | schemas/finding.yaml |
| T003 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | schemas/risk-scoring.yaml |
| T004 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | schemas/compensating-controls.yaml |

**Quality Gate**: All 4 schema files parse as valid YAML. New fields documented with types and enums. No breaking changes to existing fields.

---

### Wave 1: Baseline Infrastructure
**Effort**: M (Medium) | **Quality Gate**: Baseline detection functional | **Depends on**: Wave 0

| Task | Agent | Parallel? | File Target |
|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| T005 | senior-backend-engineer | No (sequential) | .claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md |
| T006 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes (after T005) | .claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/SKILL.md |
| T007 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes (after T005) | templates/tachi/output-schemas/threats.md |

**Quality Gate**: Orchestrator can detect and parse baseline files. Running /threat-model without a baseline produces output identical to current behavior (backward compatibility).

---

### Wave 2: Stable Re-Scan (US1 Core)
**Effort**: M (Medium) | **Quality Gate**: Zero drift on unchanged code | **Depends on**: Wave 1

| Task | Agent | Parallel? | File Target |
|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| T008 | senior-backend-engineer | No (sequential) | .claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md |
| T009 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes (after T008) | .claude/agents/tachi/risk-scorer.md |
| T010 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes (after T008) | .claude/agents/tachi/control-analyzer.md |
| T011 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes (after T008) | .claude/skills/tachi-risk-scoring/SKILL.md |
| T012 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes (after T008) | .claude/skills/tachi-control-analysis/SKILL.md |

**Quality Gate**: Pipeline re-run on unchanged code produces identical output. SC-001 (100% ID stability), SC-002 (zero drift), SC-003 (zero count drift) validated.

---

### Wave 3: Story Entry Points (US2 + US3 + US6)
**Effort**: M (Medium) | **Quality Gate**: All three story foundations in place | **Depends on**: Wave 2

| Task | Agent | Parallel? | File Target |
|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| T013 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md (RESOLVED detection) |
| T016 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md (isolated discovery) |
| T031 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/agents/tachi/risk-scorer.md (governance fields) |

**Quality Gate**: RESOLVED detection classifies correctly. Isolated discovery context excludes full finding text. Governance fields preserved for persisting findings.

**Note**: T013 and T016 both target orchestrator.md but modify distinct sections (carry-forward vs Phase 2 discovery). Can be authored in parallel as additive changes to different sections.

---

### Wave 4: Story Completion (US2 + US3 + US6 Remaining)
**Effort**: L (Large) | **Quality Gate**: All three stories functional | **Depends on**: Wave 3

| Task | Agent | Parallel? | File Target |
|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| T014 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | templates/tachi/output-schemas/threats.md |
| T015 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md |
| T017 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md |
| T018 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/agents/tachi/risk-scorer.md |
| T019 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/skills/tachi-risk-scoring/SKILL.md |
| T020 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/SKILL.md |
| T032 | senior-backend-engineer | Yes | .claude/skills/tachi-risk-scoring/SKILL.md |

**Quality Gate**: New findings discovered with [NEW] and sequential IDs. No duplicates of baseline findings. Scores bounded within +/-1.0. RESOLVED findings marked correctly. Governance fields persisted. SC-004, SC-006 validated.

**Critical Task**: T017 (merge/dedup) is the most algorithmically complex task in the entire feature. Monitor closely.

**MVP CHECKPOINT**: After Wave 4, Phases 1-5 are complete. Run lightweight validation (T033 first test case: run twice on unchanged code, verify zero drift) before proceeding. This is the recommended point to validate core functionality.

## Quality Gates Summary

| Gate | After Wave | Validates | Blocking? |
|------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| Schema Validity | Wave 0 | YAML parsing, field documentation | Yes |
| Backward Compatibility | Wave 1 | No-baseline behavior unchanged | Yes |
| Zero Drift | Wave 2 | SC-001, SC-002, SC-003 | Yes |
| Story Foundations | Wave 3 | RESOLVED detection, isolated discovery, governance fields | Yes |
| Core Functionality | Wave 4 | SC-004, SC-006, merge/dedup correctness | Yes |
| **MVP Checkpoint** | **Wave 4** | **Lightweight end-to-end validation** | **Yes** |
| Annotation Completeness | Wave 5 | All outputs annotated, SARIF baselineState | Yes |
| Coverage Gate | Waves 6-7 | SC-005, targeted re-analysis | Yes |
| Full Validation | Wave 8 | SC-001 through SC-009, all quickstart scenarios | Yes |

---

## Architect Checkpoint Schedule

Per /aod.build protocol, architect checkpoints occur at critical phases:

| Checkpoint | After Wave | Focus |
|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Checkpoint 1 | Wave 2 | Carry-forward algorithm correctness, score inheritance logic |
| Checkpoint 2 | Wave 4 | Merge/dedup algorithm, similarity threshold, bounded scoring |
| Checkpoint 3 | Wave 7 | Coverage gate design, full pipeline flow review |

---

## Execution Notes

1. **Wave structure is the execution guide**, not the phase structure. Phases are logical groupings; waves are the execution plan.
2. **Orchestrator.md is the critical file**. 8 tasks modify it across 6 waves. Validate holistically at each checkpoint.
3. **MVP checkpoint after Wave 4** is strongly recommended. Run T033 first test case before proceeding to P1 features.
4. **T035 moved to Wave 5** (from original Wave 8). No dependency on later phases.
5. **Wave 4 is the complexity peak**. 7 parallel tasks including T017 (merge/dedup). Monitor closely for rework needs.
6. **Parallel tasks within a wave target independent files**. No merge conflicts expected within any single wave.
